title: neolamarckia macrophylla
intro: Neolamarckia macrophylla, also known as the Praeok Rakam tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in Southeast Asian countries, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Neolamarckia macrophylla include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions.
